Consider the following UNIX command:

sort<in>temp; head-30<temp; nm temp

Which of the following functions shall be performed by this command?



UGC NET CSE | June 2013 | Part 2 | Question: 49
options: Sort, taking the input from “temp”, prints 20 lines from temp and delete the file temp
Sort eh file “temp”, removes 30 lines from temp and delete the file temp
Sort, taking the input from “in” and writing the output to “temp”, then prints 30 lines from temp on terminal. Finally “temp” is removed
Sort, taking the input from “temp”, and then prints 30 lines from “temp” on terminal. Finally “temp” is removed.
questions.correct_answer: Sort, taking the input from “in” and writing the output to “temp”, then prints 30 lines from temp on terminal. Finally “temp” is removed

UGC NET CSE | January 2017 | Part 2 | Question: 46

At which of the following stage(s), the degeneracy do not occur in transportation problem?

(\( m,n \) represents number of sources and destinations respectively)

  1. While the values of dual variables \( u_{i} \) and \( v_{j} \) cannot be computed
  2. While obtaining an initial solution, we may have less than \( m+n-1 \) allocations
  3. At any stage while moving towards optimal  solution, when two or more occupied cells with the same minimum allocation become unoccupied simultaneously.
  4. At a stage when the no, of \( + \)ve allocation is exactly \( m+n-1 \).


UGC NET CSE | January 2017 | Part 3 | Question: 69

With respect to a loop in the transportation table, which one of following is not correct?



UGC NET CSE | January 2017 | Part 3 | Question: 68
options: Every loop has an odd no.of cells and atleast \( 5 \).
Closed loops may or may not be  square in shape.
All the cells in the loop that have a plus or minus sign, except the starting cell, must be occupied cells.
Every loop has an even no.of cells and atleast four.
questions.correct_answer: Every loop has an odd no.of cells and atleast \( 5 \).

UGC NET CSE | June 2023 | Part 2: 67

Suppose a circular queue of capacity ( \( \mathrm{n}-1) \) elements is implemented with an array of \( \mathrm{n} \) elements. Assume that the insertion and deletion operations are carried out using REAR and FRONT as array index variable respectively. Initially,

\( \mathrm{REAR}=\mathrm{FRONT}=0 \). The conditions to detect queue empty and queue full are

1. EMPTY: REAR ==F R O N T
FULL : \( ( \) REAR+1) \( \bmod \text{n}== \) FRONT

2. EMPTY: \( ( \) FRONT +1\( ) \bmod \text{n}== \) REAR
FULL: REAR+\( 1 \) \( \bmod \mathrm{n}== \)FRONT

3. EMPTY REAR+\( 1 \)) \( \bmod \text{n} \)==FRONT
FULL: REAR \( == \) FRONT

4. EMPTY: REAR== FRONT
FULL: \( ( \) FRONT+1) \( \bmod \text{n}== \) REAR
